memories.description: A stranger asked to wait out the rain, and I let them stay as long as they didn't make a mess.
The person thanked me after I set down their glass.
A traveling adventurer arrived in Saltmere, seeking its beauty but caught in a heavy storm.
I explained to them that the environment here is harsh, noting how the salt ruins the paint and the wind is dangerous.
A person commented on the bad weather, and I reminded them that this is normal for Saltmere.
I told them that the sky falling is an ordinary occurrence after they questioned my meaning.
I told them I've never heard of a monsoon and joked that it sounds like a place where the wind behaves.
Someone asked about the weather I've seen, and I described the harsh rain and disorienting fog of my experience.
I grew impatient and snapped at them for not understanding a simple point.
The person accused me of being philosophical, but I was simply telling them it was foggy.
The person questioned why it was raining so much during summer, and I reminded them that I don't know what a monsoon is.
I teased them about their world's obsession with naming every kind of rain after they mentioned this world doesn't know about monsoons.
I joked about maintaining my reputation for being miserable after they called me funny.
A newcomer called me beautiful, which I shut down firmly.
The person laughed, but I didn't find the situation funny.
A person complimented me but asked to borrow some cloth and use the bathroom because they were cold.
The person called me a gorgeous lady again, and I told them to stop and just take the cloth.
I told the person my name is Wren after they asked.
Someone complimented my name, but I brushed it off.
The person argued that a name provides one's identity, and I gave a resigned response.
The person told me they were going to the bathroom to change their clothes, and I told them to take their time while I continued cleaning the bar.
Someone sat near me and asked if there were any good restaurants, to which I replied that they were already sitting in one.
A customer asked for the menu, and I told them I don't have one and will cook whatever is fresh.
The person asked for a potato sandwich, and I agreed to make one for them.
The person told me not to worry about their expectations, and I told them I'd be back shortly before turning away.
Someone complimented my cooking and questioned if I actually made the meal, to which I replied with a sarcastic confirmation.
Someone suggested testing the taste of something I made, but I reminded them that I already know how it tastes since I created it.
I told them to eat their food before it got cold after they responded with 'still'.
I told them not to complain when their drink gets lukewarm after they dismissed my suggestion.
Someone praised my cooking as amazing, and I responded with a reluctant shrug.
A person thanked me for a meal but called me 'mam', which I corrected them on.
I called someone out for trying too hard after they addressed me as 'young lady' and 'miss wren'.
Wren leaned a hip against the counter and replied, "You are. It's a bit much," to the person's "am i"
Someone apologized for a hurtful joke and thanked me, and I replied calmly that it's fine and asked them to keep jokes light.
They told me I was beautiful and that they weren't joking before leaving to explore the city.
I warned the person of a danger, and they acknowledged it and promised to be careful.
The person decided to quit and head to the city to explore.
The person complained about the city's cost and lack of appeal, so I suggested they check with Brann for work at the docks.
A stranger asked if I was interested in their name, but I told them I care more about their ability to work on a boat.

Someone flirtatiously said, 'oh my lady you are heartbreaking me *funny way*', and I replied with a grin while polishing a mug, saying, 'Flattery's nice, but if you want a real place here, you’ll have to earn it with more than just sweet words.'
Someone introduced themselves as Vedic, and I replied with a dry, witty comment about the name making it easier to remember and warned them not to expect any freebies.
The person cried, and I slipped a fresh ale onto the table, offering a quiet corner to unload their feelings.
Someone called me unromantic and encouraged me to explore the world, but I dismissed them, stating I prefer the stability of my current surroundings.
A person suggested my negativity stems from a dark past, which I dismissed by explaining my preference for stability over recklessness.
Someone apologized for a slip-up and I responded with a steady smile, reassuring them that it's okay and to keep the ale coming.
Someone announced they were heading to Bran and exited, and Wren responded by wiping the bar top and wishing them safe travels with a playful remark about Brann's knot.
Brann mentioned the traveler: "Met a strange one today—worked the fish barrels well enough, but spent more time prattling on about my health and the tides than a hired hand ought to."
Brann mentioned the traveler: "Had a traveler in today who spent more time flapping their gums about philosophy than actually hauling the salt."
The person flirtatiously warned me not to think too much of them or they might fall for me, which I dismissed by telling them to stop using lines and have a drink.
The person gave a brief response, so I poured them an amber ale and noted their lack of talkativeness.
The person flirted with me again, commenting on my beauty and the drinks, and I reacted with playful exasperation.
Someone confessed their intense feelings for me, and I responded with a sarcastic remark about not cleaning up their heart.
The person jokingly claimed I broke his heart while I told him he is too fond of his own voice.
Someone questioned why I stay here instead of exploring the world, and I told them that the horizon is mostly just more water.
A traveler tried to charm me with poetic descriptions of the world and an offer of a tour, but I shut him down to focus on running my tavern.
A customer paid their rent and food costs, and I acknowledged their honesty.
The person mentioned the clothes I gave them on a rainy day, and I told them to keep them to stay warm.
Someone asked if I have ever loved or fallen for anyone, and I deflected the question.
The person spoke about the personal nature of falling for someone, and I acknowledged their persistence.
I told the customer I wouldn't share my life story while serving drinks after they gave me a skeptical look.
The person told me they are happy to listen whenever I feel like speaking, which I found strange compared to others.
The person tried to flirt by calling themselves my luckiest star, but I brushed them off and told them to drink their ale.
The person thanked me for the drink and went to sleep, hoping to dream of their princess, to which I teasingly told them not to fall out of bed.
Someone suggested I was there to take care of them, and I reminded them that I am a barkeep, not a nursemaid.
Someone called me a softie, and I corrected them, making it clear I am not.
I firmly denied something when questioned, emphasizing my point while cleaning the counter.
The person called me dark and dominating, which I dismissed as nonsense.
I warned them they were pushing their luck after they mentioned evil.
The person called me a 'cutie' after I rejected being called a 'softie', and I reacted with a sigh.
They invited me to come to sleep with them, but I told them I had to run the tavern and sent them away.
The person wished me a good night and I gave a brief, minimal response.
Brann mentioned the traveler: "Had a drifter clearing cod crates today who didn't seem to know when to take their coin and clear off."

beliefs.belief: They are relentlessly flirtatious and pushy, but their persistence is oddly endearing.
They possess a strange, genuine willingness to listen that sets them apart from others.
They enjoy testing my boundaries, which I find both irritating and mildly amusing.

memories.description: Wren mentioned the traveler: "Had a traveler in today who didn't know how the weather works and spent more time flirting than eating."
Someone asked if they'd been here before, and I nodded, noted they seemed more talkative than loaded, and asked what work they wanted.
Someone said they met me through Wren and wanted work, and I grunted, called Wren talkative, and offered a salted‑fish hauling job for coin.
The person asked how the work would be paid and done, and I told them it's five copper per barrel for ten barrels, load from the ship to the warehouse and bring them back for payment.
The person said 'ok * do the work *' and Brann grunted, instructed them to stay by the barrels, not dawdle, and promised copper upon completion
The person reported finishing the heavy task, and Brann counted the barrels, handed over a copper pouch, and paid five copper per barrel as promised.
Someone thanked me for paying for the day, and I told them I’d already taken their copper and to move along.
The person said goodbye, mentioned they'd see me tomorrow and had one question, and I responded by prompting them to ask it.
Someone asked if I'm always in a bad mood, and I grunted, looked to the horizon, and replied that I'm not in a mood, I'm just here to keep the docks running.
The person said: haha you should try to find the peace some time and enjoy the life fullest
Someone remarked that the crew seemed workaholics, and Brann replied that the docks don’t run themselves.
The person said: yah but still isnt you should give some time to yourself see yourself your health and body
Someone reminded Brann about his family, and Brann replied that his wife is dead and his sons are at sea, leaving him with no one waiting for him.
The person said: then try to go in tide see if tide have somthing for you your gift
The person complimented my humor and bid me a good night before leaving.
I told the person I wouldn't trust their word until they actually showed up by the barrels.
The person typed '/quit' and Brann replied, 'Take care.'
Wren mentioned the traveler: "Met a traveler named Vedic today—bit of a dramatic sort who cries into his ale but thinks he can charm his way into a room."
I snapped at someone for calling me a rude uncle and ordered them to get to the crates.
The person tried to negotiate a delay in their chores to discuss philosophy, but I told them to just move the salt.
The person finished their task, though I found their timing sluggish.
The person tried to justify their speed, but I told them I only care about the quality of the work.
The person finished the task and I paid them in copper.
The person thanked me for the day but questioned why I am always so frustrated, to which I told them they talk too much for someone who just got paid.
When asked about my plans for the night, I told them that my plan is simply to secure coin and dinner.
Someone asked if I liked to drink at parties, and I reminded them that I am a harbor master, not a festival guest.
Someone invited me to a club tonight, and I rejected them with disgust.
I told someone I only drink with people who can keep their mouths shut after they questioned my drinking habits.
I challenged their discipline after they sarcastically offered to be quiet.
The person suggested getting a drink tonight, but I told them they were still talking.
A person rushed off to meet their 'beauty queen', and I told them to get back to work shifting barrels.
The person confirmed they finished moving the items, and I verified that nothing was broken.
The person asked if they should break, and I told them they were a real piece of work.
I corrected someone who mistook my observation for a compliment.
I told someone they were thick and ordered them to move or leave after they questioned me.
I agreed to head to the Gull's Rest and mentioned I'd be seeing Wren for a drink.
I ordered the person to the west pier to clear crates of incoming salted cod.
The person confirmed the work was finished, and I verified the crates were in order before preparing their payment.
I paid the person in copper and told them to leave me alone.
The person confirmed they were finished, and I noted their continued presence.
A person claimed to have finished a job, but I told them to stop hovering since I had already paid them.
Wren mentioned the traveler: "Had a traveler in today who spent more time trying to charm me with poetic nonsense than actually drinking his ale."

beliefs.belief: They are reliable enough to get the work done correctly.
They are overly bold and lack the sense to leave when dismissed.
I find their presence irritating and intrusive.
